subtitle: A wild 500-meter golden beach under dark schist cliffs on Portugal's last undeveloped Atlantic coast, the Costa Vicentina — a premier surf break beneath the Pedra da Agulha needle rock, guarded by a 17th-century Portuguese fort and a rare 12th-century Islamic Sufi ribat.
spike_statement: A wild 500 m beach under schist cliffs on Portugal's last undeveloped Atlantic coast — a premier surf break guarded by a 17th-century fort and a rare 12th-century Sufi ribat.

levers.photo_tone: wave-hammered Atlantic surf and butterscotch sand curving between towering dark schist cliffs, the thin Pedra da Agulha pinnacle offshore, the ruined fort on the headland, surfers as small marks on a wild coast
